subtitle: Up to 254 kilometers of unbroken, windswept sand from the jetties of the Rio Grande seaport to the Uruguay border — widely called the world's longest beach: cold-watered, drivable, and anchored by a 1976 shipwreck.
spike_statement: The world's longest beach: up to 254 km of unbroken sand from the Rio Grande port to the Uruguay border, so long it serves as a road, on cold Falklands-current water.

levers.photo_tone: an immense flat windswept beach vanishing to the horizon under a wide grey-blue southern sky, the stone jetties of the Molhes reaching into a cold South Atlantic, tyre tracks and a rusting hull on hard sand
